evidence_quotes.quote: I think that we should have to confirm the subscriptions for them to continue

selling data to 3rd-parties should be illeagal or very hard to do, if a sell like that is ever to happen I should have full control of my data and where and to whom it goes because I can agree to googles policy but I dont know the policies of the 3rd-parties which might kick off a selling chain which ends up with me having thousands of spam emails hit me every month.
lifetime options with no plan to support them for a lifetime

paragraphs.text: Subscriptions dark patterns - I think that we should have to confirm the subscriptions for them to continue, this would get rid of the need to search for cancellation pages, any charges because of the cancellation (because its clear that I agreed to only one time frame for now), no need to contact support or search the web to find how I can get rid of a subscription. I can leave it alone and it would cancel itself. This would definetely help the customers keep track of them, not spend the money on something they dont need etc. Unclear policies - Each website, company, checkbox, app etc should have a very clear summary of the things I agree to, I shouldnt have to read through pages and pages of policies on what can happen, there should also be a limit on how long the policies can be to stop the companies from putting everything there worded in every kind of way possible, the policies language should be clear and easilly understood by anyone that uses the app - and so for apps used by underage people, everything should also be clear to them I also think that selling data to 3rd-parties should be illeagal or very hard to do, if a sell like that is ever to happen I should have full control of my data and where and to whom it goes because I can agree to googles policy but I dont know the policies of the 3rd-parties which might kick off a selling chain which ends up with me having thousands of spam emails hit me every month. I also support Stop Killing Games and think that if possible, all digitally sold stuff should have an end-of-life plan, as there are still a lot of companies out there selling lifetime options with no plan to support them for a lifetime
